thesis: Franco-Nevada is the largest listed gold-focused royalty company: it earns cash from gross-metal-equivalent ounces on third-party mines without operating mines or mine-level capital spending. FY2025 operating cash flow was $1.49 billion on 519k GEOs; Q1 2026 set new records with $520 million operating cash flow and $3.4 billion available capital, with no debt.
why_market_wrong: Predictive attribute: quality_premium_compression. The market may treat FNV as passive gold beta at a peak multiple and underweight Cobre Panamá restart, M&A optionality, and oil-linked diversified revenue.

key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share

open_questions: Cobre Panamá stockpile processing timing and 2027 GEO contribution vs 2026 guidance exclusion.
Normalize Q1 2026 CRA refund ($49.5M) and Cascabel buy-back gains for owner-cash growth path.
Populate SEC EDGAR folder; IR harvest only today.
Human review: development inventory proof uses aggregate milestone judgment, not asset-level trees.
